name: Uncooked Fondant/Candy Variations
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter or margarine in a saucepan over low heat. Stir in milk, vanilla, and salt.
2. Remove from heat; gradually add powdered sugar.
3. Turn out onto a board sprinkled with powdered sugar and work with hands until smooth.
Yield: 2 cups fondant for making candies.
1. Drain maraschino cherries thoroughly. Cover each cherry completely by shaping about 2 teaspoons of fondant around it. Use 1 cup fondant for 24 cherries.
2. Melt chocolate using a package of German chocolate and semi-sweet chocolate chips with melted paraffin.
3. Drop fondant creams one at a time into the melted chocolate, then remove with a fork onto waxed paper.
4. Chill well. Yield: 24 chocolate-covered cherries.
1. Split and remove pits from 24 dates. Fill each date cavity with about 1 teaspoon of fondant.
2. Use 1/2 cup fondant for 24 dates.
3. Shape half a cup of fondant into one-inch balls, then roll each ball in cinnamon.
Yield: About 2 dozen cinnamon balls.
4. Blend half a cup of fondant, 1/2 teaspoon peppermint extract, and 2 drops of red food coloring with hands to form teaspoons.
Shape the mixture into flat patties about the size of a teaspoon.
Yield: 24 peppermint patties.
5. Mix half a cup of fondant with 1/4 cup peanut butter and 2 tablespoons corn syrup until combined.
6. Drop spoonfuls onto waxed paper to form drops about an inch across.
Yield: About 20 peanut butter drops.
